title: Objects, Classes, Namespaces, and Binding · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: Model a library circulation domain while tracing real Python object and attribute behavior. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in an inspectable library domain model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reason about object identity, type, value, mutability, equality, and lifetime in an object-oriented design.
Define classes and create instances while distinguishing class objects from the instances they construct.
Trace class, instance, module, and local namespaces during attribute lookup and assignment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
